little_template_justification: The introduction opens with a clear puzzle or anomaly: citizenship renunciation is increasingly accepted, but only when paired with emigration. It then reframes that anomaly as a two-horned dilemma and resolves it by rejecting mandatory citizenship for long-term residents and defending renunciation without emigration.
intro_presents_main_result_quote: In this article, I argue that citizens should have the right to renounce their citizenship without the requirement of emigration.
intro_prior_update_quote: In this article, I argue that citizens should have the right to renounce their citizenship without the requirement of emigration.
section_titles: SETTING THE STAGE: TERMS AND DEFINITIONS
CITIZENSHIP RENUNCIATION AS A FORM OF EXPRESSIVE POLITICAL EXIT
